articles.title: Delhi Govt Orders 1-4 PM Break For Outdoor Workers Amid Heatwave - BW Businessworld
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Bihar
articles.full_text:
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has announced a series of coordinated measures to protect residents from the ongoing heatwave, with a specific focus on labourers and outdoor workers who face the highest exposure to extreme temperatures.
Highlighting the serious health risks posed by prolonged exposure to heat, the Chief Minister said that all workers in both the private and public sectors must be given a mandatory break between 1 PM and 4 PM, the peak heat hours. The directive aims to reduce direct sun exposure and prevent heat-related illnesses, such as heatstroke and dehydration, among those working outdoors for extended periods.
As part of efforts to ensure adequate hydration for the public, the government has arranged drinking water facilities across key public spaces. Water provisions have been introduced on buses operated by the Delhi Transport Corporation, while volunteers are being deployed at bus shelters to distribute water and Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) to passengers. These steps are designed to provide immediate relief to daily commuters affected by the extreme weather.
The healthcare system in the city has also been placed on alert. Hospitals have been stocked with essential medicines and are prepared to handle cases related to heat stress. Cooling areas are being created within hospital premises to offer quick respite to patients suffering from heat-related conditions.
In schools, authorities have been directed to ensure the availability of drinking water and ORS for students to safeguard children during school hours. Additionally, volunteers are distributing caps and cotton mufflers at multiple locations to help residents shield themselves from the harsh sun.
The Chief Minister stressed that public awareness and timely precautions remain critical in reducing the impact of the heatwave and urged residents to remain cautious during peak daytime hours.

articles.title: 65 km wind, heavy rains, thunderstorm: IMD issues weather warning, Delhi, Noida, Bihar, UP, Rajasthan to..., orange alert for 20 states, check full list and IMD forecast - news24online.com
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Bihar
articles.full_text:
65 km wind, heavy rains, thunderstorm: IMD issues weather warning, Delhi, Noida, Bihar, UP, Rajasthan to…, orange alert for 20 states, check full list and IMD forecast
The IMD has issued a weather alert in as many as 20 states. According to the IMD Weather Warning, heavy rains, powerful winds and thunderstorms will hit Delhi, Noida, Bihar, UP, Rajasthan among other states on May 4 (Monday).
The IMD has issued a weather alert in as many as 20 states. According to the IMD Weather Warning, heavy rains, powerful winds and thunderstorms will hit Delhi, Noida, Bihar, UP, Rajasthan among other states on May 4 (Monday).
Rain, dust storms to hit Delhi-NCR
As per the IMD weather warning, light to moderate rain accompanied by dust storms is expected to hit Delhi and its adjoining NCR districts Noida, Ghaziabad, Faridabad and Gurugram on Monday (May 4), while powerful, gusty winds with speeds up to 40 to 50 kilometers per hour are also predicted. Temperatures are expected to drop by 2 to 3 degrees, bringing some relief from the scorching heat. However, the sudden storm is likely to disrupt power supply in these areas.
IMD predicts torrential rains in Uttar Pradesh
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Uttar Pradesh is likely to witness particularly harsh weather with a torrential rain alert has been issued for dozens of districts, including Meerut, Agra, Varanasi, Prayagraj, Kanpur, and Lucknow. The IMD has forecast lightning, hailstorms and gusty winds in parts of the state, though temperatures are expected to drop to around 34 degrees Celsius.
IMD issues orange alert in 20 states
The IMD has issued a weather warning for more than 20 states, including Delhi-NCR, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, Noida, Gurugram, and Madhya Pradesh, while heavy rains are forecast across the northeast and southern India. According to the IMD forecast, seven cyclonic circulations are active in various parts of the country, and a western disturbance is active near the border with Pakistan, which will trigger heavy rains and thunderstorms in many parts of the country. The weather agency has issued 'Orange' and 'Yellow' alerts in more than 20 states.
Heavy rains, hailstorms in Bihar; windstorm in MP
According to the IMD weather warning, heavy rains, accompanied by thunderstorm and strong winds with speeds up to 60kmph, are expected in several districts of Bihar, while neighboring Jharkhand will witness similar stormy weather conditions. The IMD issued hailstorm alert for the Raisen, Vidisha, Bhopal, and Gwalior divisions in Madhya Pradesh, where gusty winds of up to 60 kmph are expected. Moderate to heavy rainfall is forecast in the eastern and southern parts of the state.
IMD warns of 'thundersqualls', powerful winds in several states
The IMD has warned that several states will experience "thundersquall" conditions on with wind speeds reaching 50 to 60 kilometers per hour. As per the IMD weather alert, thundersqualls are likely to hit parts of Bihar, Chhattisgarh, coastal Andhra Pradesh, and eastern Rajasthan, while powerful winds are expected to strike in Gangetic West Bengal, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Punjab, and Vidarbha.

articles.title: 4 May 2026 Weather Update: मौसम का बदला मिजाज. प्रचंड गर्मी के बीच Delhi-NCR के साथ-साथ 19 राज्यों में बारिश से राहत, साथ ही आंधी-तूफान का अलर्ट - gnttv.com
articles.search_term: (लू OR "लू चलना" OR "भीषण गर्मी" OR "प्रचंड गर्मी" OR "कड़ी धूप" OR "तेज धूप" OR "गर्म हवा" OR "उष्ण लहर" OR "ताप लहर" OR "हीट वेव" OR "झुलसाने वाली गर्मी" OR "तपती गर्मी" OR "भयंकर गर्मी" OR "गर्मी का कहर" OR "गर्मी का प्रकोप" OR "अत्यधिक गर्मी" OR मौसम OR "मौसम अपडेट" OR "मौसम का हाल" OR "मौसम पूर्वानुमान" OR "मौसम अलर्ट" OR आईएमडी OR "आईएमडी पूर्वानुमान" OR "आईएमडी अलर्ट" OR "तापमान में बढ़ोतरी" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR "गर्मी से निजात") Bihar
articles.full_text:
देश में एक बार फिर मौसम करवट लेने जा रहा है. 4 मई को कई राज्यों में बारिश और तेज आंधी का असर देखने को मिलेगा. मौसम विभाग ने साफ संकेत दिए हैं कि ये बदलाव सिर्फ बारिश तक सीमित नहीं रहेगा, बल्कि कई शहरों में तेज हवाओं के साथ-साथ तूफान की स्थिति भी बन सकती है.
19 राज्यों में अलर्ट जारी
मौसम विभाग के मुताबिक यूपी, बिहार, दिल्ली, झारखंड, हरियाणा, राजस्थान, पंजाब, उत्तराखंड, हिमाचल, जम्मू-कश्मीर, मध्य प्रदेश, छत्तीसगढ़, पश्चिम बंगाल, ओडिशा, असम, मेघालय, तमिलनाडु, कर्नाटक और आंध्र प्रदेश समेत 19 राज्यों में बारिश और आंधी की चेतावनी जारी की गई है. कई इलाकों में हवा की रफ्तार 70 से 80 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे तक रह सकती है.
दिल्ली-NCR में अलर्ट
राजधानी दिल्ली में 4 मई यानी आज तेज बारिश और आंधी की संभावना मौसम विभाग द्वारा जताई गई है. तापमान में गिरावट जरूर आएगी, लेकिन तेज हवाएं लोगों की परेशानी बढ़ा सकती हैं. यहां अधिकतम तापमान करीब 32 डिग्री और न्यूनतम 22 डिग्री रहने का अनुमान है.
यूपी में कई जिलों में असर
उत्तर प्रदेश के सहारनपुर, मेरठ, अलीगढ़, वाराणसी, प्रयागराज समेत कई जिलों में तेज बारिश और आंधी का अलर्ट है. हवा की रफ्तार 50 से 60 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे तक जा सकती है. लखनऊ में तापमान 29 से 23 डिग्री के बीच बने रहने की उम्मीद है.
बिहार-झारखंड में भारी बारिश का खतरा
बिहार के पटना, मुजफ्फरपुर, दरभंगा, पूर्णिया जैसे जिलों में मूसलाधार बारिश की चेतावनी दी गई है. वहीं झारखंड के कई हिस्सों में भी मध्यम से भारी बारिश और तेज हवाएं चल सकती हैं. इन राज्यों में बिजली गिरने की भी आशंका जताई गई है. लोगों को घर से निकलते वक्त सावधानी बरतनी जरूरी होगी.
पहाड़ी राज्यों में खास सतर्कता जरूरी
उत्तराखंड और हिमाचल प्रदेश में भी मौसम बिगड़ने वाला है. कई जिलों में तेज बारिश और 60-70 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से हवाएं चल सकती हैं. ऐसे में यात्रा करने वाले यात्रियों को खास सावधान रहने की सलाह दी गई है.
किसानों और यात्रियों के लिए अलर्ट
मौसम विभाग ने खास तौर पर किसानों और नदी-डैम के पास रहने वाले लोगों को सतर्क रहने को कहा है. मछुआरों को भी समुद्र या नदी में जाने से बचने की सलाह दी गई है. अगर आप कहीं बाहर जाने का प्लान बना रहे हैं, खास कर अलर्ट वाले राज्यों में तो मौसम की जानकारी एक बार जरूर चेक कर लें. 4 मई का दिन कई राज्यों के लिए मौसम के लिहाज से भारी रहने वाला है. ऐसे में सावधानी ही सबसे बड़ी सुरक्षा साबित होती है.
